title: London Transport 1938/56/59/62 Stock
description: A Collection Of My 1938 And 1956 And 1959 And 1962 Stocks
body:
The London Underground 1938 / 1962 Tube Stock!
The 1938 Stock Was Built as the Final Product of a Small Protoype Batch of Tube Trains Made in the 1920’s. The 1938 Stocks Design Was the One That Got Chosen and So They Were Mass Produced and Could Be Seen on Multiple Lines, Lasting Until the 70s or 80s in Regular Operation! Their Design Became the Backbone of All Tube Trains up Until the 1970s!

The 1962 Stock Is One of the Trains Based of off the 1938 Stocks Design, Having Relatively Small Differences but Noticeable Ones Like Different Light Configuration and Having an Unpainted Aluminium Bodyshell as Opposed to the 1938 Stock’s Red Bodyshell.

Guard Panels Added To All 1938 Stock Varients (currently show in all cars but will be fixed soon

1938 And 1956 Stock Headlights Have Been Altered Again

Included in This Pack Is:

1938 Stock (Orange-Y Pink-Y Roof)

1938 Stock (Black Roof)

1938 Stock (Gray Roof)

1962 Stock (Regular)

1962 Stock (Rail Adhesion Train)

1938 Stock (Island Line Livery)

1956 Stock (prototype 1959 stock)

1959 Stock (Silver) (just a 62 stock with the headlights flipped)

1959 Stock (Heritage Red And Cream)

1959 Stock (London Underground Corporate Livery)

1962 Stock (Lighter Grey Roof)

Many Thanks To Extrememakerx For Allowing Me to Use the 1992 Stocks Bogies and Couplers!
Many Thanks To Curt For Making The Sound Set For These Trains!
